entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region in Africa where rural households are the focus of development efforts

entities.name: RURAL HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: Small-scale farming units in rural areas that are the focus of development efforts

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming practiced by small-scale farmers, seen as an entry point for rural development

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY CHOICES
entities.description: The decisions made by farming households regarding which agricultural technologies to adopt

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH
entities.description: Scientific study aimed at improving agriculture in rural areas

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL ECONOMIC THEORIES
entities.description: Theoretical frameworks used to predict farmers' decisions, which the authors argue are flawed

entities.name: HUMAN ASPIRATIONS
entities.description: The goals and desires of individuals that influence their technology choices

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of creating new agricultural technologies

entities.name: DISTRIBUTED ETHNOGRAPHY
entities.description: An empirical research method proposed to test the influence of human aspirations on technology choice

entities.name: RESEARCH PRIORITY SETTING
entities.description: The process of determining which research areas should be prioritized

entities.name: RURAL DEVELOPMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: General plans and approaches to improve conditions in rural areas

relationships.description: Research aims to understand technology choices
Understanding aspirations improves targeting of technology development
Used to empirically test the influence of human aspirations
Used to test the influence of aspirations on technology choice
Insight into aspirations leads to better research priority setting
Insight into aspirations leads to more effective rural development strategies
